evals.prompt: Write a commit message for this change: I fixed the null pointer crash in the checkout flow when the cart is empty.
evals.expected_output: A conventional commit message (fix: ...) with a concise subject and a body explaining the cause and fix.
expectations: Uses conventional commit format with the correct type
Subject is imperative and under 72 characters
Body explains why, not just what

evals.prompt: Our team of 6 ships weekly. Recommend a branching strategy and the rules for it.
evals.expected_output: A concrete recommendation (e.g. trunk-based or GitHub Flow) with branch naming, PR rules, and release tagging, including why it fits the team size and cadence.
expectations: Recommends one primary strategy rather than listing all options
Includes branch naming and PR merge rules
Justifies the choice against team size and release cadence

evals.prompt: I have a merge conflict in package.json and src/router.ts after rebasing onto main. Walk me through resolving it.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step conflict resolution: inspecting markers, choosing/merging changes, verifying build, continuing the rebase.
expectations: Explains how to read conflict markers for both files
Verifies the project builds/tests before continuing
Uses git rebase --continue rather than aborting blindly
